structure.title: Project Structure
structure.description: View the project folders, layer responsibilities, and the location of authentication and access control logic.
structure.treeDescription: The comments beside each folder provide a quick map for developers seeing the project for the first time.
structure.roleDescription: These descriptions explain why each folder is separated and define where code should belong.
structure.flowDescription: This is the basic path from a page request to the Backend API.

folders.api: Manages Axios baseURL, timeout, common headers, and authentication interceptors. It attaches access tokens and handles token refresh and request retry after a 401 response.
folders.assets: Stores images and static files imported by the interface. Separating resources from components makes replacements and reuse locations easier to find.
folders.components: Stores reusable UI pieces. common contains app-wide elements such as AuthProvider, layout defines the shell, ui owns shadcn/ui source, and users contains user and account menu components.
folders.config: Stores shared application policies such as role-based allowed paths. access.ts is shared by the Sidebar and AccessRoute so menu visibility and direct URL access follow the same rule.
folders.hooks: Exposes TanStack Query and React state in a form pages can consume, such as useHealth and useUsers. Hooks delegate HTTP work to services so pages do not know request details.
folders.locales: Stores Korean and English text in language-specific JSON files. UI text stays out of components so adding a language or changing wording does not change screen code.
folders.mocks: Stores MSW handlers with the same URLs and response shapes as the real Backend. Login, token refresh, logout, role access, and token expiration can be tested without a server.
folders.pages: Stores route-level screen components. Authentication and access result pages such as LoginPage and ForbiddenPage are also composed in this layer.
folders.routes: Manages URL-to-page connections, login checks in ProtectedRoute, and role checks in AccessRoute. Unauthorized access goes to 403 and unknown addresses go to 404.
folders.services: Stores feature API calls through modules such as HealthService, UserService, and AuthService. Authentication screens delegate HTTP work to AuthService instead of using Axios directly.
folders.stores: Uses Zustand for shared client state such as the theme and current authentication. Access tokens remain in memory instead of browser storage, while TanStack Query owns server response caches.
folders.styles: Stores global Tailwind styles, Light/Dark Theme variables, and the base font. Screens use shared theme values instead of hard-coded colors.
folders.types: Stores TypeScript contracts such as UserDto, HealthDto, and AuthDto for API requests, responses, and user roles. Authentication, access control, services, and UI share the same data shapes.
folders.utils: Stores small pure functions that do not belong to a feature or screen, such as cn and formatDateTime. Only genuinely reused functions belong here.

flow.page: Builds the screen and uses hooks.
flow.hook: Connects screen logic and TanStack Query.
flow.service: Handles response mapping and shared business rules.
flow.api: Uses Axios for Backend requests, attaches access tokens, and refreshes authentication before retrying a request after a 401 response.

libraries.title: Project Libraries
libraries.description: View the libraries currently used by the project and the technologies planned for later.

core.title: Frontend Foundation
core.description: Core technologies for running, developing, and building the project.

ui.title: UI and Styling
ui.description: Libraries used to build the visual design and shared UI.

data.title: State and Data
data.description: Libraries for client state, server data, and API communication.

app.title: Application Support
app.description: Libraries for routing, translations, and the base font.

planned.title: Planned Libraries
planned.description: Libraries to add when the related feature requirements are confirmed.

items.react: Builds screens and state changes with reusable components.
items.typescript: Checks data contracts for props, API responses, and state.
items.vite: Runs the development server and Production Build quickly.
items.tailwind: Builds utility styles and CSS Variable based themes.
items.shadcn: Provides accessible project-owned UI such as Avatar, Dropdown Menu, Select, and Card that can be adjusted to the product design.
items.lucide: Provides consistent icons for the theme, sidebar, and other UI.
items.classTools: Combines conditional Tailwind classes and resolves conflicts.
items.cva: Manages typed style variants for shared components.
items.zustand: Keeps the theme, access token, current user, and authentication readiness in memory.
items.query: Manages server cache, loading, and error states, and clears data from the previous user after logout or session expiration.
items.axios: Handles Backend requests, adds the access token header, refreshes authentication after a 401 response, and retries the original request.
items.msw: Mocks login, token refresh, logout, and role-based APIs so authentication flow and token expiration can be tested without a Backend.
items.router: Connects URLs, pages, and the shared layout while ProtectedRoute and AccessRoute enforce login and role-based screen access.
items.i18n: Manages Korean and English text and runtime language changes.
items.geist: Provides the project font without an external CDN.
items.animate: Provides Tailwind animations required by shadcn/ui components.
items.table: Manages sorting, filtering, and pagination for the user list.
items.signalr: Will receive real-time alarms and state change events.
items.echarts: Will build dashboard charts and visualizations.
